selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to understand the reasons why people create and share false information. Some may do so for attention, while others may have more sinister motives. Regardless of the reason, identifying and addressing the root cause of the issue is key. One effective strategy is to use fact-checking tools and sources to verify the accuracy of content before sharing it. Additionally, promoting media literacy education can help individuals develop critical thinking skills and become more discerning consumers of information. Finally, social media platforms must take responsibility for policing their platforms and removing fake content to maintain a safe and trustworthy environment for users."
,"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, understanding the reasons why it is created is crucial. Verifying content before sharing, promoting media literacy education, and relying on fact-checking tools are all effective strategies. Social media platforms must also take action to remove false information from their platforms.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": []
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: To effectively manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to understand the reasons why people create and share false information. Some may do so for attention, while others may have more sinister motives. Regardless of the reason, identifying and addressing the root cause of the issue is key. One effective strategy is to use fact-checking tools and sources to verify the accuracy of content before sharing it. Additionally, promoting media literacy education can help individuals develop critical thinking skills and become more discerning consumers of information. Finally, social media platforms must take responsibility for policing their platforms and removing fake content to maintain a safe and trustworthy environment for users.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, understanding the reasons why it is created is crucial. Verifying content before sharing, promoting media literacy education, and relying on fact-checking tools are all effective strategies. Social media platforms must also take action to remove false information from their platforms.
